characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Adaptational Angst Downgrade: InGear, all three of them lived in aCrapsack Worldand often had to watch people die around them, and that's not even going into their individual traumas and neuroses. InCatscratch, the cats have none of these issues and live relatively carefree lives. Big, Thin, Short Trio: Big (Gordon), thin (Waffle) and short (Blik). Comic Trio: While stated in the front page, they share anEd, Edd n Eddyfamiliarity with having a fallible andout of touch with realitylike Schemer,a only sane manwho is powerless (even if Gordon can be the motivational or effective at times) and thedingbatwhodoes whatever he's told. Disembodied Eyebrows: Gordon and Waffle have these. Furry Ear Dissonance: Mr. Blik and Waffle both have long stylized ears that arenothinglike real cat ears. In Waffle's case, they are floppy and droop down backwards.

characters.name: Mr. Blik
characters.description: Adaptational Jerkass: He wasn't as assholish in the original comic as he is in the show. SeeCharacter Exaggeration. Adaptation Name Change: He was named Mr. Black inGear. Anti-Role Model: Blik's not someone who should be looked up to or imitated. Big Brother Bully: He's prone to insulting, hitting, and bossing around Gordon and Waffle a lot. Big, Thin, Short Trio: He's the Short to Gordon's Big and Waffle's Thin.

characters.name: Gordon
characters.description: Big Little Brother: Younger than Mr. Blik, but taller. Big, Thin, Short Trio: He's the Big to Waffle's Thin and Mr. Blik's Short. Cannot Spit It Out: When it comes to Human Kimberly. Cartoon Creature: We don't know breed he is besides that he's either a manx or bobtail. Catchphrase: "Great Gopher!", "Aye!", "In the name of the Highland Quid Clan", "Cheer!" and "Foul creature..."

characters.name: Waffle
characters.description: Anti-Villain: When he starts acting "evil" because Blik told him he was in "EVIL!", he's still rather amiable. The Baby of the Bunch: The youngest of the cat brothers, and the one who acts the most like it. Berserk Button: Waffle is very vocal about his dislike of liars. While normally goofy and laid-back, he will often lash out whenever he's lied to. Usually, it's directed towards Mr. Blik. He also hates cheaters. Big Little Brother: The biggest of the brothers and the youngest. Big, Thin, Short Trio: He's the Thin to Gordon's Big and Mr. Blik's Short.

characters.name: Hovis
characters.description: Mrs. Cramdilly's English butler that her cats inherited Deadpan Snarker: Especially to Mr. Blik, though it's hard to blame him. Dreadful Musician: On the tuba. Hidden Depths: He's a hidden genius, even building his own robot to make his job easier. The Jeeves: The butler who frequently plays theOnly Sane Manto the cats.

characters.name: Human Kimberly
characters.description: All Girls Like Ponies: As long as they're unicorns, that is. Ambiguously Brown: Her skin is slightly dark, but her ethnicity is unclear. Berserk Button: She Invoked the rules for''Girls Only'' sleepoverinSlumber Party. Cheerful Child: A child, and an optimist at that. Childish Tooth Gap: To signify her youth.

characters.name: Squeakus
characters.description: Arch-Nemesis: by the end of his episode, it is established he becomes one for Waffle. The Dreaded: Just hearing his name is enough to get Gordon and Mr. Blik to scream in terror. Faux Affably Evil: He presents himself as a friendly guy to Waffle, but he is actually a sadistic rodent who is using him to dispose of three cats in one go.

characters.name: Kraken
characters.description: Benevolent Genie: Despite initially trying to eat the cats, he shows to be honorable when Gordon beats him granting his wish to restore Blik's tail at no cost.

characters.name: Katilda
characters.description: Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal: She wears two purple ribbons and a matching headband on her brunet hair. Actor Allusion: This isn'tthe only yellow catHynden Walchhas voiced. Always Someone Better: To Blik. The Big Girl: She is strong and energetic. Girlish Pigtails: She has her hair in pigtails, but she's far from girly.

characters.name: Barkmeat
characters.description: Beware of Vicious Dog: Barkmeat is a scary looking pitbull with a rather dark, aggresive mood. Big Shadow, Little Creature: How Barkmeat is shown first when Waffle meets him. He still is not to be messed with. Card-Carrying Villain: Barkmeat is from a human viewpoint a standard, homeless, troublesome dog, but he plays up this tropeto convince himself that he is more important and untouchable than he really is. Diabolical Dog Catcher: His view of dog catchers in general. His wariness is not completely unfounded - while Barkmeat is able to bully bystanders, a dogcatcher is trained to deal with him and possibly would end up putting to sleep a dog with a personality like Barkmeat's. The Dreaded: Barkmeat's attempts of making himself known seem to work well - Blik and Gordon immediately know who he is, once they see him, and show their fear.

characters.name: Audrey
characters.description: Furry Confusion: She is the only cat in the show who is a lower animal. Informed Species: Well, fur coloring, anyway. A calico doesn't look like she does. Missing Mom: So far, we only see her in a photo of the trio when they were kittens.Ghosts of Cramdillyimplies she passed away (or, otherwise, left the mansion) some years before the show. Really Gets Around:Truth in Television, as cats are a rather promiscuous species. That said, the show mentions they trio have the same mom, but it mentions nothing about their dads. Strong Family Resemblance: We see a little of her in each of her kittens: Mr. Blik inherited her ears, Gordon got her patch on his eye, and Waffles got her stripes.
